procedure.name: Atomic Red Team validation for T1574.009
procedure.description: Automated emulation procedure for T1574.009 executed via Invoke-AtomicRedTeam inside the Metatron lab environment.

command: Import-Module '/opt/invoke-atomicredteam/Invoke-AtomicRedTeam.psd1' -ErrorAction Stop; $env:PathToAtomicsFolder='/opt/atomic-red-team/atomics'; try { bash -lc "if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive; apt-get update -y >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; apt-get install -y inetutils-telnet ldap-utils lsof mlocate smbclient samba-common-bin dnsutils >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi; if ! command -v b64encode >/dev/null 2>&1 && command -v base64 >/dev/null 2>&1; then printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\nbase64 "$@"\n' > /usr/local/bin/b64encode && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/b64encode; fi" } catch { Write-Host '[WARN] dependency bootstrap failed'; }; try { Invoke-AtomicTest T1574 -PathToAtomicsFolder '/opt/atomic-red-team/atomics' -TestNumbers 1 -GetPrereqs -ErrorAction Continue } catch { Write-Host '[WARN] GetPrereqs failed for T1574'; }; Invoke-AtomicTest T1574 -PathToAtomicsFolder '/opt/atomic-red-team/atomics' -TestNumbers 1

sigma.title: Hijack Execution Flow - Dynamic Linker Hijacking via LD_PRELOAD on Linux
Hijack Execution Flow - Path Interception via Unquoted Service Path
Potential Initial Access via DLL Search Order Hijacking
Regsvr32 DLL Execution With Uncommon Extension
Potential Registry Persistence Attempt Via DbgManagedDebugger

osquery.name: t1574_009_crontab
osquery.query_text: SELECT event, minute, hour, day_of_month, month, day_of_week, command, path FROM crontab;

osquery.name: t1574_009_startup
osquery.query_text: SELECT name, path, source, status, type FROM startup_items;

osquery.name: t1574_009_suid
osquery.query_text: SELECT path, username, groupname, permissions FROM suid_bin;

story.narrative: Execution, telemetry, detections, artifacts, network observations, and SOAR response are tracked as separate witnesses and tied together through shared anchors.

promotion.reason: Full S5 validation: 0 reproducible real sandbox runs across ≥3 executions, 8 Sigma rules matched with event linkage, 3 osquery correlations, raw telemetry preserved, analyst reviewed, clean baseline.
